title: Design within execution limits
description: Account for timeout, memory, payload-size, and concurrency limits explicitly instead of discovering them under load.
body: Use when a function calls downstreams, processes large inputs, or runs under bursty load. Do not set a timeout shorter than the slowest downstream call, and do not push large payloads through invocation limits. Actions: set the timeout above worst-case downstream latency with margin, size memory to the work (CPU scales with memory on Lambda), stream or offload large payloads to object storage, and reserve/limit concurrency to protect downstreams. Validation signals include functions completing before timeout under load tests and no throttling errors. Risk boundary: a timeout shorter than a downstream call causes silent partial work and retries; an unbounded concurrency burst can overwhelm a database — set reserved concurrency on risky paths.
